Logical Model: OBDSMeldung - Detailed Descriptions

Draft as of 2026-05-04

Definitions for the obds-meldung logical model.

  • Key Elements Table
  • Differential Elements Table
  • Snapshot Elements Table

Guidance on how to interpret the contents of this table can be foundhere

0. obds-meldung
Definition

Logisches Modell einer oBDS-Meldung (Onkologischer Basisdatensatz v3.0.5). Bildet die XML-Struktur einer einzelnen Meldung ab, wie sie von Meldestellen an die Krebsregister übermittelt wird.

ShortoBDS Meldung
Control0..*
Is Modifierfalse
Logical ModelInstances of this logical model are not marked to be the target of a Reference
2. obds-meldung.meldungID
Definition

Eindeutige Kennung der Meldung (XML-Attribut Meldung_ID)

ShortMeldung_ID
Control1..1
Typestring
Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
4. obds-meldung.melderID
Definition

Kennung des Melders (XML-Attribut Melder_ID)

ShortMelder_ID
Control1..1
Typestring
Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
6. obds-meldung.meldebegruendung
Definition

Grund der Meldung: I=Behandlung, A=Diagnose, V=Verlauf, T=Tod

ShortMeldebegruendung
Control1..1
Typecode
Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
8. obds-meldung.eigeneLeistung
Definition

Ob die gemeldeten Daten auf eigener Leistung beruhen (J/N/U)

ShortEigene Leistung
Control1..1
Typecode
Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
10. obds-meldung.tumorzuordnung
Definition

Zuordnung der Meldung zu einem bestimmten Tumor des Patienten

ShortTumorzuordnung
Control1..1
TypeBackboneElement
Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
12. obds-meldung.tumorzuordnung.id
Definition

Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

ShortUnique id for inter-element referencing
Control0..1
Typestring
Is Modifierfalse
XML FormatIn the XML format, this property is represented as an attribute.
Summaryfalse

    Definition

    May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants.                           Definition

                          May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants.                                                                                     Definition

                                                                                    ShortTodesursache_ICD
                                                                                    Control0..*
                                                                                    TypeBackboneElement
                                                                                    Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
                                                                                    764. obds-meldung.tod.todesursachen.id
                                                                                    Definition

                                                                                    Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

                                                                                    ShortUnique id for inter-element referencing
                                                                                    Control0..1
                                                                                    Typestring
                                                                                    Is Modifierfalse
                                                                                    XML FormatIn the XML format, this property is represented as an attribute.
                                                                                    Summaryfalse
                                                                                    766. obds-meldung.tod.todesursachen.extension
                                                                                    Definition

                                                                                    May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

                                                                                    ShortAdditional content defined by implementations
                                                                                    Comments

                                                                                    There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

                                                                                    Control0..*
                                                                                    TypeExtension
                                                                                    Is Modifierfalse
                                                                                    Summaryfalse
                                                                                    Alternate Namesextensions, user content
                                                                                    Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
                                                                                    ext-1: Must have either extensions or value[x], not both (extension.exists() != value.exists())
                                                                                    SlicingThis element introduces a set of slices on obds-meldung.tod.todesursachen.extension. The slices areUnordered and Open, and can be differentiated using the following discriminators:
                                                                                    • value @ url
                                                                                      • 768. obds-meldung.tod.todesursachen.modifierExtension
                                                                                      Definition

                                                                                      May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

                                                                                      Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

                                                                                      ShortExtensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized
                                                                                      Comments

                                                                                      There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

                                                                                      Control0..*
                                                                                      TypeExtension
                                                                                      Is Modifiertrue because Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them
                                                                                      Summarytrue
                                                                                      Requirements

                                                                                      Modifier extensions allow for extensions that cannot be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the definition of modifier extensions.

                                                                                      Alternate Namesextensions, user content, modifiers
                                                                                      Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
                                                                                      ext-1: Must have either extensions or value[x], not both (extension.exists() != value.exists())
                                                                                      770. obds-meldung.tod.todesursachen.code
                                                                                      Definition

                                                                                      ICD-10-Code der Todesursache

                                                                                      ShortCode
                                                                                      Control1..1
                                                                                      Typecode
                                                                                      Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension
                                                                                      772. obds-meldung.tod.todesursachen.version
                                                                                      Definition

                                                                                      ICD-10-Version

                                                                                      ShortVersion
                                                                                      NoteThis is a business version Id, not a resource version Id (see discussion)

                                                                                      Typestring
                                                                                      Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension

                                                                                      Guidance on how to interpret the contents of this table can be foundhere

                                                                                      0. obds-meldung
                                                                                      Definition

                                                                                      Logisches Modell einer oBDS-Meldung (Onkologischer Basisdatensatz v3.0.5). Bildet die XML-Struktur einer einzelnen Meldung ab, wie sie von Meldestellen an die Krebsregister übermittelt wird.

                                                                                      ShortoBDS Meldung
                                                                                      Logical ModelInstances of this logical model are not marked to be the target of a Reference
                                                                                      2. obds-meldung.meldungID

                                                                                      0. obds-meldung
                                                                                      Definition

                                                                                      Logisches Modell einer oBDS-Meldung (Onkologischer Basisdatensatz v3.0.5). Bildet die XML-Struktur einer einzelnen Meldung ab, wie sie von Meldestellen an die Krebsregister übermittelt wird.

                                                                                      14. obds-meldung.tumorzuordnung.extension
                                                                                      Definition

                                                                                      May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

                                                                                      ShortAdditional content defined by implementations
                                                                                      Comments

                                                                                      There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

                                                                                      Control0..*
                                                                                      TypeExtension
                                                                                      Is Modifierfalse
                                                                                      Summaryfalse
                                                                                      Alternate Namesextensions, user content
                                                                                      Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
                                                                                      ext-1: Must have either extensions or value[x], not both (extension.exists() != value.exists())
                                                                                      SlicingThis element introduces a set of slices on obds-meldung.tumorzuordnung.extension. The slices areUnordered and Open, and can be differentiated using the following discriminators:
                                                                                      • value @ url
                                                                                        • 16. obds-meldung.tumorzuordnung.modifierExtension
                                                                                        Definition

                                                                                        May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

                                                                                        Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

                                                                                        ShortExtensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized
                                                                                        Comments

                                                                                        There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

                                                                                        Control0..*
                                                                                        TypeExtension
                                                                                        Is Modifiertrue because Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them
                                                                                        Summarytrue
                                                                                        Requirements

                                                                                        Modifier extensions allow for extensions that cannot be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the definition of modifier extensions.

                                                                                        Alternate Namesextensions, user content, modifiers
                                                                                        Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
                                                                                        ext-1: Must have either extensions or value[x], not both (extension.exists() != value.exists())

Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

                                                                                                            Comments

                                                                                                            There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions.                                                                                                             TypeExtension
                                                                                                            Is Modifierfalse

                                                                                                            • value @ url
                                                                                                              • 232. obds-meldung.diagnose.modulMamma.modifierExtension
                                                                                                              Definition

                                                                                                              May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

                                                                                                              Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

                                                                                                              ShortExtensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized
                                                                                                              Comments

                                                                                                              There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

                                                                                                              Control0..*
                                                                                                              TypeExtension
                                                                                                              Is Modifiertrue because Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them
                                                                                                              Summarytrue
                                                                                                              Requirements

                                                                                                              Modifier extensions allow for extensions that cannot be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the definition of modifier extensions.

                                                                                                              Alternate Namesextensions, user content, modifiers
                                                                                                              Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
                                                                                                              ext-1: Must have either extensions or value[x], not both (extension.exists() != value.exists())

To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

                                                                                                                              • 388. obds-meldung.op.komplikationen.modifierExtension
                                                                                                                              Definition

                                                                                                                              May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

                                                                                                                              Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

                                                                                                                              ShortExtensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized
                                                                                                                              Comments

                                                                                                                              There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

                                                                                                                              Control0..*
                                                                                                                              TypeExtension
                                                                                                                              Is Modifiertrue because Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them
                                                                                                                              Summarytrue
                                                                                                                              Requirements

                                                                                                                              Modifier extensions allow for extensions that cannot be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the definition of modifier extensions.

                                                                                                                              Alternate Namesextensions, user content, modifiers
                                                                                                                              Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
                                                                                                                              ext-1: Must have either extensions or value[x], not both (extension.exists() != value.exists())

                                                                                                                                      456. obds-meldung.syst.extension
                                                                                                                                      Definition

                                                                                                                                      May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

                                                                                                                                      ShortAdditional content defined by implementations
                                                                                                                                      Comments

                                                                                                                                      There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

                                                                                                                                      Control0..*
                                                                                                                                      TypeExtension
                                                                                                                                      Is Modifierfalse
                                                                                                                                      Summaryfalse
                                                                                                                                      Alternate Namesextensions, user content
                                                                                                                                      Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
                                                                                                                                      ext-1: Must have either extensions or value[x], not both (extension.exists() != value.exists())
                                                                                                                                      SlicingThis element introduces a set of slices on obds-meldung.syst.extension. The slices areUnordered and Open, and can be differentiated using the following discriminators:
                                                                                                                                      • value @ url
                                                                                                                                        • 458. obds-meldung.syst.modifierExtension
                                                                                                                                        Definition

                                                                                                                                        May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

                                                                                                                                        Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

                                                                                                                                        ShortExtensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized
                                                                                                                                        Comments

                                                                                                                                        There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

                                                                                                                                        Control0..*
                                                                                                                                        TypeExtension
                                                                                                                                        Is Modifiertrue because Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them
                                                                                                                                        Summarytrue
                                                                                                                                        Requirements

                                                                                                                                        Modifier extensions allow for extensions that cannot be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the definition of modifier extensions.

                                                                                                                                        Alternate Namesextensions, user content, modifiers
                                                                                                                                        Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
                                                                                                                                        ext-1: Must have either extensions or value[x], not both (extension.exists() != value.exists())

                                                                                                                                                  • 540. obds-meldung.st.bestrahlungen.modifierExtension
                                                                                                                                                  Definition

                                                                                                                                                  May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

                                                                                                                                                  Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

                                                                                                                                                  ShortExtensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized
                                                                                                                                                  Comments

                                                                                                                                                  There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

                                                                                                                                                  Control0..*
                                                                                                                                                  TypeExtension
                                                                                                                                                  Is Modifiertrue because Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them
                                                                                                                                                  Summarytrue
                                                                                                                                                  Requirements

                                                                                                                                                  Modifier extensions allow for extensions that cannot be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the definition of modifier extensions.

                                                                                                                                                  Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
                                                                                                                                                  ext-1: Must have either extensions or value[x], not both (extension.exists() != value.exists())

                                                                                                                                                          • 618. obds-meldung.verlauf.fernmetastasen.modifierExtension
                                                                                                                                                          Definition

                                                                                                                                                          May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

                                                                                                                                                          Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

                                                                                                                                                          ShortExtensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized
                                                                                                                                                          Comments

                                                                                                                                                          There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

                                                                                                                                                          Control0..*
                                                                                                                                                          TypeExtension
                                                                                                                                                          Is Modifiertrue because Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them
                                                                                                                                                          Summarytrue
                                                                                                                                                          Requirements

                                                                                                                                                          Modifier extensions allow for extensions that cannot be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the definition of modifier extensions.

                                                                                                                                                          Alternate Namesextensions, user content, modifiers
                                                                                                                                                          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
                                                                                                                                                          ext-1: Must have either extensions or value[x], not both (extension.exists() != value.exists())

                                                                                                                                                                  • 712. obds-meldung.verlauf.modulMamma.modifierExtension
                                                                                                                                                                  Definition

                                                                                                                                                                  May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

                                                                                                                                                                  Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

                                                                                                                                                                  ShortExtensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized
                                                                                                                                                                  Comments

                                                                                                                                                                  There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

                                                                                                                                                                  Control0..*
                                                                                                                                                                  TypeExtension
                                                                                                                                                                  Is Modifiertrue because Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them
                                                                                                                                                                  Summarytrue
                                                                                                                                                                  Requirements

                                                                                                                                                                  Modifier extensions allow for extensions that cannot be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the definition of modifier extensions.

                                                                                                                                                                  Alternate Namesextensions, user content, modifiers
                                                                                                                                                                  Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
                                                                                                                                                                  ext-1: Must have either extensions or value[x], not both (extension.exists() != value.exists())

                                                                                                                                                                        Primitive ValueThis primitive element may be present, or absent, or replaced by an extension

                                                                                                                                                                        764. obds-meldung.tod.todesursachen.id
                                                                                                                                                                        Definition

                                                                                                                                                                        Unique id for the element within a resource (for internal references). This may be any string value that does not contain spaces.

                                                                                                                                                                        766. obds-meldung.tod.todesursachen.extension
                                                                                                                                                                        Definition

                                                                                                                                                                        May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

                                                                                                                                                                        ShortAdditional content defined by implementations
                                                                                                                                                                        Comments

                                                                                                                                                                        There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

                                                                                                                                                                        Control0..*
                                                                                                                                                                        TypeExtension
                                                                                                                                                                        Is Modifierfalse
                                                                                                                                                                        Summaryfalse
                                                                                                                                                                        Alternate Namesextensions, user content
                                                                                                                                                                        Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
                                                                                                                                                                        ext-1: Must have either extensions or value[x], not both (extension.exists() != value.exists())
                                                                                                                                                                        SlicingThis element introduces a set of slices on obds-meldung.tod.todesursachen.extension. The slices areUnordered and Open, and can be differentiated using the following discriminators:
                                                                                                                                                                        • value @ url
                                                                                                                                                                          • 768. obds-meldung.tod.todesursachen.modifierExtension
                                                                                                                                                                          Definition

                                                                                                                                                                          May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the element and that modifies the understanding of the element in which it is contained and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

                                                                                                                                                                          Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

                                                                                                                                                                          ShortExtensions that cannot be ignored even if unrecognized
                                                                                                                                                                          Comments

                                                                                                                                                                          There can be no stigma associated with the use of extensions by any application, project, or standard - regardless of the institution or jurisdiction that uses or defines the extensions. The use of extensions is what allows the FHIR specification to retain a core level of simplicity for everyone.

                                                                                                                                                                          Control0..*
                                                                                                                                                                          TypeExtension
                                                                                                                                                                          Is Modifiertrue because Modifier extensions are expected to modify the meaning or interpretation of the element that contains them
                                                                                                                                                                          Summarytrue
                                                                                                                                                                          Requirements

                                                                                                                                                                          Modifier extensions allow for extensions that cannot be safely ignored to be clearly distinguished from the vast majority of extensions which can be safely ignored. This promotes interoperability by eliminating the need for implementers to prohibit the presence of extensions. For further information, see the definition of modifier extensions.

                                                                                                                                                                          Alternate Namesextensions, user content, modifiers
                                                                                                                                                                          Invariantsele-1: All FHIR elements must have a @value or children (hasValue() or (children().count() > id.count()))
                                                                                                                                                                          ext-1: Must have either extensions or value[x], not both (extension.exists() != value.exists())
